title: Image layer storage/deletion slow on Oracle Linux
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When pulling images, after downloading the image layers, there is a long delay.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Pull any image, or build any image.

**Describe the results you received:**
```
> time podman pull docker.io/fedora
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob ae7b613df528 done
Copying config b3048463dc done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
b3048463dcefbe4920ef2ae1af43171c9695e2077f315b2bc12ed0f6f67c86c7

real    1m13.358s
user    0m14.674s
sys     0m6.100s
```

Truncated `strace` output. Note that after the "Storing signatures" step, the wait for pid 94832 takes 69 seconds. (I think; I'm not familiar with Linux syscalls at all.)

```
> strace -T podman pull docker.io/fedora
# ...
clone(child_stack=NULL, flags=CLONE_CHILD_CLEARTID|CLONE_CHILD_SETTID|SIGCHLD, child_tidptr=0x7fb5bc41fa10) = 94832 <0.001674>
close(7)                                = 0 <0.000030>
close(8)                                = 0 <0.000023>
wait4(94832, Trying to pull docker.io/fedora...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:ae7b613df528a37664448affa6e52ff405701cda015a2a67301423bc20226b61
Copying config sha256:b3048463dcefbe4920ef2ae1af43171c9695e2077f315b2bc12ed0f6f67c86c7
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
b3048463dcefbe4920ef2ae1af43171c9695e2077f315b2bc12ed0f6f67c86c7
[{WIFEXITED(s) && WEXITSTATUS(s) == 0}], 0, NULL) = 94832 <69.195757>
--- SIGCHLD {si_signo=SIGCHLD, si_code=CLD_EXITED, si_pid=94832, si_uid=993, si_status=0, si_utime=1450, si_stime=205} ---
rt_sigreturn({mask=[]})                 = 94832 <0.000035>
futex(0x5615ed0c8e98, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 1) = 1 <0.000044>
exit_group(0)                           = ?
+++ exited with 0 +++
```

While executing this in real time outside of `strace`, the pause takes place between the `Storing signatures` and `b30484...` lines.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected the image to save within ~10 seconds after the layers are downloaded.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This issue happened on version 1.9.x as well.

Someone mentioned that this may be due to image decompression

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Sat Nov  7 04:18:34 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
> rpm -q podman
podman-2.0.5-5.0.1.module+el8.3.0+7866+f387f528.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No, and Yes

I have checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide, and this is the latest available version on this repository. I haven't tried building 2.1.0 yet.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Oracle Linux 8 OS in VMware VM

comments.body: @mtrmac Am I correct in remembering that the next step after downloading is extracting? That seems like it could be our culprit.

Yes, in the current implementation of pull, â€ścopying blobâ€ť just decompresses the layer and writes it as a simple linear temporary file, ideally a nice efficient streaming operation. Only in the â€ścommitâ€ť step (after â€śStoring signaturesâ€ť, without a separate marker) are all the new layers actually extracted to the filesystem as individual files, incurring all the seeks and journalling filesystem commits and the associated disk queue/cache flushes.

We do want to eventually make more of the filesystem extraction parallel with downloading (at least for multi-layer images), and visible in the progress output; a significant part of that already exists as part of Valentinâ€™s work on concurrent copy detection (which also took on 3-4 other issues, some of them much more difficult).

So this is not currently very surprising (although the report does not contain much precise data; 69 seconds of commit vs. 73 seconds total would probably count as surprising).

(And if Iâ€™m reading that strace / output correctly, 69 seconds is the time for the whole subprocess, probably including the pull. Anyway, a useful strace would need to include that child process, maybe using `-f` or `-ff`.)

@mtrmac, thanks for your input!

> 69 seconds of commit vs. 73 seconds total would probably count as surprising

This is what I'm observing; the layer only takes 5-10 seconds to download, and then a minute to commit.

> the report does not contain much precise data ... a useful strace would need to include that child process, maybe using `-f` or `-ff`

I ran `strace -T -f podman pull docker.io/fedora`, but the output file is 70M. Is there somewhere you want me to post this, or any filters you want me to use? (Sorry, I'm generally unfamiliar with `strace`)

[strace-with-forks-podman-pull.txt.gz](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/5630905/strace-with-forks-podman-pull.txt.gz)

I didn't realize GitHub supported file attachments.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Does anyone have any pointers that I could use to start troubleshooting this myself? This is probably a system configuration issue or something on my end, so this may not be reproducible by others. It adds a lot of time for our container-based CI platform, so I'd like to look into it further.

Is `strace` the right tool to look into this? Should I be looking at something else?

Does the issue happen only with Oracle Linux?  Have you tried a different distro and keeping the same configuration for the VM?

Few things you can investigate:

run `top` during the decompression and see how much cpu is the podman process taking.

It may also be related to slow storage.  Could you try setting the equivalent of KVM/libvirt `cache-mode=unsafe` for the VM?

> run top during the decompression and see how much cpu is the podman process taking.

D'oh! I should have thought of that a long time ago. It looks like an antivirus agent is stalling the stall. I'll work with our sysadmins to see if we can work around this. Sorry to bother you.

Thanks for your help!
Ok I am going to close this issue.  Reopen if this turns out to be related to podman.
